nodes.name: quiz.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Handles incoming HTTP requests and responses for quiz domain — customer-facing list/start/submit/results and staff CRUD with question management.

nodes.name: tier.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Handles HTTP requests for subscription tier management (list, get, create, update, deactivate).

nodes.name: system-trash.controller.ts
nodes.summary: Handles HTTP requests for the system trash bin — list, restore, and hard-delete soft-deleted characters/contexts/quizzes.

nodes.name: quiz.service.ts
nodes.summary: Business logic for quizzes: customer browse/start/submit session flow, staff CRUD, question management. Tracks play counts, scores, answer details.

nodes.name: system-trash.service.ts
nodes.summary: System trash bin service — bulk restore and hard-delete for soft-deleted characters, historical contexts, and quizzes. Hard delete cascades to documents and chat sessions.

nodes.name: tier.routes.ts
nodes.summary: Maps /tiers endpoints — public GET, SYSTEM_ADMIN-only POST/PUT/DELETE. Includes @openapi JSDoc.

nodes.name: system-trash.routes.ts
nodes.summary: Maps /system/trash endpoints for CONTENT_ADMIN/SYSTEM_ADMIN — list, restore, and hard-delete trashed items.

layers.name: API / Routes
layers.description: Defines REST API endpoints and routes requests to controllers.

layers.name: Controllers
layers.description: Handles HTTP request/response lifecycle and input validation.

layers.name: Services
layers.description: Contains business logic and orchestrates data access.

layers.name: Models
layers.description: Data schemas and database interaction layer (ORM).

layers.name: Middlewares
layers.description: Cross-cutting concerns like Authentication, Error Handling, and Logging.

layers.name: Configuration & Setup
layers.description: Application bootstrapping, DB connection setup, and environment config.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The application starts here. It initializes the environment, establishes database connections, and starts the Express HTTP server.

tour.title: Express Configuration
tour.description: Configures the Express app instance, sets up global middlewares (CORS, body parsing, rate limiting), and mounts the main API router.

tour.title: Master API Router
tour.description: Aggregates all domain-specific routes (e.g., users, chat, documents) into a single API version prefix (like /api/v1).

tour.title: Authentication Middleware
tour.description: A crucial security layer that intercepts protected routes to verify JWT tokens and ensure the user is authenticated before reaching the controller.

tour.title: Chat Controller
tour.description: As an example of the Controller layer, this file handles incoming requests for chat operations. It extracts parameters and calls the ChatService.

tour.title: Chat Service
tour.description: The core business logic for the chat domain lives here. It handles complex operations, orchestrates multiple models, and prepares data for the controller.

tour.title: Chat Session Model
tour.description: At the bottom of the stack, the Model layer defines the database schema for chat sessions and provides the interface to query or mutate the underlying database.
